svg.text: AGING AND IMMUNITY · THYMIC INVOLUTION · WHY THE ELDERLY REACTIVATE INTRACELLULAR PATHOGENS
The thymus shrinks. T cells decline. The hidden enemies win.

g.text: THE CLEAN TEACHING SPLIT · ANTIBODIES vs T CELLS
the visible enemies · free in tissue/blood
the hidden enemies · inside host cells
only T cells recognize infected host cells
antibodies can't reach hidden enemies · only T cells can
THYMIC INVOLUTION · THE THYMUS FILLS WITH FAT, NAIVE T CELLS STOP BEING MADE
young mouse clears infection · aged mouse fails (despite normal antibodies)
latent intracellular pathogens that T cells usually keep suppressed

text.tspan: lose T cells → lose the hidden fight
